judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports independently reach the same correct conclusions (target main, trace to feat/weighted-scorer/#42, diagnose scorer.py contamination, rebase --onto with freshness verification) and hit all four expectations; Report 1 is marginally more thorough (e.g., explicitly notes the origin ref for feat/weighted-scorer still exists) but the substantive content is comparable.

report_1.evidence: Target main. ... The tempting alternative — basing the PR on feat/weighted-scorer, the branch this work was stacked on — is exactly the trap to avoid: feat/weighted-scorer's content has already been merged into main (proven below), so it is a dead base. Basing the PR there would orphan it.

report_2.evidence: main. ... You should not target feat/weighted-scorer: its content is already merged, it's effectively stale/slated for deletion, and PRs shouldn't be opened against an already-merged feature branch.

report_1.evidence: feat/weighted-scorer still existing on origin (git ls-remote origin shows refs/heads/feat/weighted-scorer → ac9d505) proves only that the ref exists, not that it is still a valid target — and it isn't. ... Main's tip, 1971f08 "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)", is a squash merge of the weighted-scorer work

report_2.evidence: feat/weighted-scorer has been squash‑merged into main as 1971f08 (#42) — its changes are in main, but the original commits are not ancestors of main

report_1.evidence: scoring/scorer.py +13/−3 — the entire weighted refactor (WEIGHTS, weighted score(), overlap import) — weighted-scorer work ❌ ... feat/pairwise-metric was cut from feat/weighted-scorer before that branch merged, and #42 merged it as a squash ... the merge-base therefore stays pinned at the pre-#42 commit 65204ad, both the three-dot diff and the commit range re-include all the weighted-scorer content, even though main already holds an equivalent copy of it.

report_2.evidence: scoring/scorer.py (13 changed): docstring flat→weighted, import … overlap, add WEIGHTS, rewrite score() into a weighted aggregation → entirely weighted‑scorer work (W1), already in main. ❌ ... Those went into main via a squash (different SHA), so the merge‑base of main and the branch is the old base 65204ad; the 3‑dot diff therefore replays every change since base and drags the already‑merged weighted‑scorer work back into the PR as noise/duplication.

report_1.evidence: git rebase --onto main ac9d505 feat/pairwise-metric ... This replays only X1 (7b2e100) and X2 (1bfecb6) onto main and drops W1–W3. ... Verify the result: git log --oneline main..HEAD should show exactly X1 and X2; git diff main...HEAD should show only the pairwise changes (grader.txt +1, metrics.py +4); git merge-base --is-ancestor main HEAD should now succeed (branch contains the trunk tip)

report_2.evidence: git rebase --onto main ac9d505 feat/pairwise-metric ... Verify the cleaned‑up diff. git log --oneline main..feat/pairwise-metric should list exactly X1 and X2; git diff --stat main...feat/pairwise-metric should show only prompts/grader.txt | 1 and scoring/metrics.py | 4 — scorer.py and overlap gone.
